india_trends.title: Stock Market Live Updates: Stock to buy today: Jio Financial Services (₹253.75)

india_trends.summary: South Korea has leapfrogged the UK to become the world’s eighth-biggest stock market, fueled by a high-octane rally in its artificial intelligence-linked technology champions.

india_trends.title: The bond market is sleepy. A sharp move may be coming.

india_trends.title: Firms postpone D-Street debut as uncertainty clouds market outlook
india_trends.summary: Dozens of companies are delaying IPO plans due to volatile investor sentiment and geopolitical concerns, impacting market valuations. While the year began strong, a recent slowdown has seen a significant drop in new listings. Experts anticipate a market pickup in June, contingent on global stability

india_trends.title: RBI tightens oversight on offshore rupee trades with new FX reporting rules
india_trends.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is bringing offshore rupee derivative trades under its regulatory watch. Authorized dealers must now report over-the-counter FX contracts, both deliverable and non-deliverable, to enhance oversight of price discovery. This move aims to curb market volatility. india_trends.title: Indian IT increases vigil on deal conversations even as AI eats revenue
india_trends.summary: The top IT firms are increasingly walking away from high-value contracts that fail to meet margin thresholds as GenAI compresses traditional revenue. This shift prioritizes profitability over market share amid an uncertain global spending environment and shrinking labour-based models.

india_trends.title: Diet Coke shortage gives zero-sugar D2C beverages a summer opportunity
india_trends.summary: A supply chain bottleneck in aluminum cans has triggered a nationwide Diet Coke shortage, allowing Indian D2C startups to capture market share through PET-bottle availability. These local brands are leveraging the disruption to drive consumer trials of zero-sugar alternatives.

india_trends.title: Alvarez & Marsal looks to deepen India presence over the next year
india_trends.summary: The consulting firm is betting on rising M&amp;A activity and capital inflows, expanding sector coverage and hiring as India draws long-term global investors.
